concerns.text: Task T009 states the classifier trains on 'validated static logs (proxy)' and explicitly notes 'NOT ablation data directly'. This violates the data-flow dependency established by T008 (Ablation) and T014 (Proxy Validation). The plan and spec require the model to be trained on ablation-derived ground truth (or the proxy ONLY if validated as a substitute for ablation). T009 consumes the wrong artifact (proxy logs) instead of the validated ground truth labels generated by T008/T008b, breaking the semantic dependency chain required by Constitution Principle VI.

Task T008 specifies performing the ablation study on 'a subset' of data, but the plan requires a 'full ablation study' on the training set to generate ground-truth labels for the classifier. T008b (Extractor) and T009 (Classifier) depend on the output of T008. If T008 only processes a subset, the downstream tasks (T009) will lack the necessary training data volume, creating a semantic gap between the plan's requirement and the task's execution scope.

Task T016 defines the error condition for exceeding the token budget as 'fallback to full layers'. This contradicts the data-flow logic of the Dynamic Policy (US1) which requires the system to 'truncate or prune' layers to stay within the 4096 token budget. The task description implies a failure mode that violates the hard constraint defined in the spec, breaking the logical flow of the dynamic policy implementation.

Task T025 implements McNemar's test unconditionally. The plan explicitly states that if trajectories diverge (non-deterministic), McNemar's is invalid and a 'Permutation Test or Z-test' is required. The task list lacks a preceding task or conditional logic to detect trajectory divergence and route to the appropriate statistical test, creating a dependency gap where the wrong statistical method may be applied to the data.

Task T005 mentions handling NaN/Inf edge cases but does not explicitly define the data-flow consequence (fallback to 'all-layers' retrieval and logging) mandated by the spec's edge case handling. While the task is parallel-safe, the description is vague on the specific output artifact or state change required when the edge case is triggered.
